_schema.description: A batch is a group of 25 contacts selected around a shared hypothesis: that contacts with a specific combination of institution type, title tier, asset size, and geography will respond to a particular positioning angle and communication approach. Each batch maps to exactly one sequence. The sequence is the instrument built to test the hypothesis. One batch, one hypothesis, one sequence. If the hypothesis changes, it is a new batch.

fields.sequenceId: The seq-NNN this batch is linked to. One-to-one, permanent.
fields.hypothesis: The 'we believe' statement. Why this contact group should respond to this angle.

qualifiers.institutionType: community bank | credit union | CDFI | other
qualifiers.titleTier: The specific title range this batch targets

fields.angle: The core positioning the sequence is built around
fields.apolloListId: Apollo list ID once contacts are loaded
fields.enrolledDate: Date contacts were enrolled in Apollo
fields.completedDate: Date the sequence finished running for this batch

result.notes: What worked, what didn't, what to change

batches.hypothesis: Community bank VP/EVP leaders in the Southeast managing $500M–$1B institutions are losing inherited deposits silently at life transition moments and have no product or process addressing it. They will respond to a sequence that names the invisible attrition problem and positions Prismm as infrastructure, not a pitch.
batches.angle: Invisible attrition — deposit relationships lost when the primary account holder dies and the institution has no system to retain the beneficiary relationship
